name: Tesla Optimus
short_description: Tesla's general-purpose humanoid robot, first revealed in 2022 and iterated since.
long_description: A bipedal humanoid Tesla positions for repetitive or unsafe labor, leveraging perception and control work from its vehicle program. Still pre-commercial, it is a prominent example of the humanoid form being used to signal future general-purpose capability.

images.caption: Tesla Optimus on display in a Tesla showroom.
images.alt_text: A Tesla Optimus humanoid robot standing on display in a Tesla showroom.

sources.title: Tesla — We, Robot
sources.access_notes: Reveal year 2022; year field reflects first reveal — see Open Questions.

interactions.title: Sort battery cells on its own, fixing its own slips
interactions.description: On a factory bench Optimus picks 4680 cells and orients them into a tray run entirely by an on-board neural net on the FSD computer, using only cameras and the force and tactile sensors in its hands — and self-corrects when a cell goes in crooked.

interactions.title: Fold a shirt on a folding table
interactions.description: In a Tesla clip the robot pinches a wrinkled shirt off a table and folds it into a neat square — a deliberately fiddly manipulation task, driven by teleoperation to collect training data rather than running fully autonomously.

interactions.title: A dance routine, balanced on two legs
interactions.description: Tesla released footage of Optimus running through ballet-like leg raises, the running man, and pirouettes without toppling — a public demonstration of whole-body balance and limb coordination on the humanoid frame.

interactions.title: Tend bar at a launch party — with a human behind the curtain
interactions.description: At the 2024 'We, Robot' event, guests filmed Optimus units pouring drinks, dancing, and playing rock-paper-scissors; reporters later confirmed Tesla staff were remotely teleoperating the bots' interactions the whole night.

interactions.title: Step aside for a coworker on the floor
interactions.description: Sharing aisle space with people and moving carts, the robot reroutes its walk around a coworker who steps into its path — grounded in its legged mobility and vision-based navigation already shown moving through Tesla factory spaces.

interactions.title: Feel for the bolt before it cranks down
interactions.description: Seating a fastener it can't fully see, Optimus uses the force and torque sensing in its arms to detect the moment of contact and back off rather than stripping the thread — an extension of the force or torque input it already carries.

interactions.title: Hand a tool across the bench when asked
interactions.description: A worker says 'pass me the 10 mil' and the robot picks the right tool out of a cluttered tray and holds it out handle-first, leaning on the vision-language-action model it runs to map spoken intent to a grasp.
